rationale: The citing paper uses Mizik (2010) to support a core framing claim that ROA is a short-term metric that can miss the long-term effects of marketing actions such as innovation. This point is used to justify one of the paper's stated contributions and its choice to evaluate outcomes with forward-looking Tobin's q (and risk), rather than relying on ROA alone. Although cited alongside Rust et al. (2004), the citation plays a clear conceptual role in the paper's performance-measure setup rather than being a purely incidental mention.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Mizik (2010) as conceptual support for the argument that short-term accounting performance can be myopic for marketing outcomes. This framing underpins the paper's move to assess CEO ideology effects using long-term firm value and risk measures.
